seoTitle: Fix a Missing 1095-A E-File Reject in TurboTax
metaDescription: Getting an e-file reject over a missing Form 1095-A in TurboTax? Here's how to identify the cause and fix it so your health insurance return goes through.
intro: A missing 1095-A rejection is one of the more frustrating e-file errors because it stops your return cold until it's resolved. This guide explains what's causing the reject, why Form 1095-A matters for anyone who received health insurance premium tax credits, and the steps to correct the issue in TurboTax so your return can be resubmitted successfully.

steps.heading: Understand what Form 1095-A is for
steps.body: Form 1095-A reports health insurance coverage information, particularly for filers who received premium tax credits, and TurboTax needs this data to process your return.

steps.heading: Identify why the reject is happening
steps.body: Common causes behind the missing 1095-A e-file reject are covered so you can pinpoint what triggered the error on your specific return.

steps.heading: Walk through the fixing process step by step
steps.body: Follow the correction steps in TurboTax to add or fix the missing 1095-A information and clear the reject.

steps.heading: Apply tips to avoid future e-file rejects
steps.body: A few preventive habits are shared to help you avoid running into similar e-file rejections in future tax seasons.

faq.question: Why is TurboTax rejecting my e-file over a missing 1095-A?
faq.answer: This happens when the IRS or TurboTax detects that health insurance marketplace coverage information tied to premium tax credits wasn't properly included in your return.

faq.question: What is Form 1095-A used for?
faq.answer: Form 1095-A provides details about your health insurance coverage through the marketplace, which is essential if you received premium tax credits during the year.

faq.question: Can I still e-file after fixing this error?
faq.answer: Yes, once you correct the missing 1095-A information following the steps in TurboTax, you can resubmit your return for e-filing.

faq.question: How do I avoid this e-file reject next year?
faq.answer: The video shares tips for avoiding future e-file rejects, generally centered on double-checking that all health insurance forms are entered before submitting.
